name: Coconut Oatmeal Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Place oats in a bowl. Add boiling water and let stand 20 minutes.
2. In a large bowl, cream the butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
3. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then stir in the vanilla.
4. Gradually add the rolled oats to the mixture, stirring until combined.
5. In a separate bowl, whisk together the dry ingredients (flour, baking soda, cinnamon, nutmeg, salt).
6. Pour the dry ingredient mixture into the oat mixture and stir until just combined.
7. Grease an 8-inch cake pan with butter and sprinkle with flour to prevent sticking.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top with a spatula.
9. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 40 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
10. Remove from the oven and let cool slightly.
11. Prepare the topping by creaming the remaining butter with sugar until smooth.
12. Stir in the cream, nuts, and shredded coconut until well combined.
13. Spread the topping evenly over the warm cake.
14. Broil the cake for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until the topping is golden brown and set.
15. Remove from the oven and garnish with apple confit and taffy apples, if desired.
16. Serve warm or at room temperature.
Confittu of Apples:
1/2 cup Unsalted butter
1 cup Sugar
2 tbsp Brandy
2 tsp Lemon juice
4 Granny Smith apples, peeled, cored, and halved
1. In a sauté pan over medium heat, melt the butter.
2. Add sugar to the melted butter and stir occasionally until it begins to turn golden brown and caramelized.
3. Stir in the brandy and lemon juice.
4. Place the apple pieces into the caramel mixture and cook slowly until they are well coated and tenderized.
5. Serve warm alongside the Coconut Oatmeal Cake.
